Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft|12=draft}} and was formerly ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ({{ill|Jinushigami|ja|地主神|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|12=draft|12=draft}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|12=draft|12=draft}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|12=draft|12=draft}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|12=draft|12=draft}} |- |{{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=彦火火出見尊|12=draft|12=draft}} |yes |yes |yes |- |{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=豊玉姫尊|12=draft|12=draft}} |yes |yes |yes |- |[[Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} |yes |no |no |- |[[Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=玉依姫尊|12=draft|12=draft}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=塩司翁・猿田彦命|12=draft|12=draft}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|12=draft|12=draft}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}" entry for March 20, 860 ({{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft}} era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|12=draft|12=draft}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near {{ill|Sakurajima|ja|桜島|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|12=draft|12=draft}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|12=draft|12=draft}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|12=draft|12=draft}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzaisha]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]]

Toyouke-no-Ōmikami |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft|12=draft}}
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=draft|12=draft}}, Hirairi |Alias = Ujise Shrine |Fudasho = Kagoshima Sansha |Annual Festival = October 18 and February 18 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima city#Japan Kagoshima }} [[File:Sangoku meisyo zue Ujise shirine.jpg|thumb|Edo-period illustration of Ujise Shrine (from volume 3 of {{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|12=draft|12=draft}})]] '''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sōmuta|ja|草牟田|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture. Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft|12=draft}} and was formerly ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ({{ill|Jinushigami|ja|地主神|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|12=draft|12=draft}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|12=draft|12=draft}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|12=draft|12=draft}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|12=draft|12=draft}} |- |{{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=彦火火出見尊|12=draft|12=draft}} |yes |yes |yes |- |{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=豊玉姫尊|12=draft|12=draft}} |yes |yes |yes |- |[[Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} |yes |no |no |- |[[Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=玉依姫尊|12=draft|12=draft}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=塩司翁・猿田彦命|12=draft|12=draft}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|12=draft|12=draft}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}" entry for March 20, 860 ({{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft}} era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|12=draft|12=draft}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near {{ill|Sakurajima|ja|桜島|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|12=draft|12=draft}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|12=draft|12=draft}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|12=draft|12=draft}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzaisha]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]]

Amatsuhidaka-Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto
Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto
Toyouke-no-Ōmikami |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}, Hirairi |Alias = Ujise Shrine |Fudasho = Kagoshima Sansha |Annual Festival = October 18 and February 18 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima city#Japan Kagoshima }} [[File:Sangoku meisyo zue Ujise shirine.jpg|thumb|Edo-period illustration of Ujise Shrine (from volume 3 of {{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会}})]] '''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sōmuta|ja|草牟田}}, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture. Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and was formerly Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ({{ill|Jinushigami|ja|地主神}}) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)}} |- |{{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=彦火火出見尊}} |yes |yes |yes |- |{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=豊玉姫尊}} |yes |yes |yes |- |[[Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}} |yes |no |no |- |[[Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=玉依姫尊}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=塩司翁・猿田彦命}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}" entry for March 20, 860 ({{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near {{ill|Sakurajima|ja|桜島}}'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}, notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City}}, and {{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮}} in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzaisha]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]]

revision.comment: 75 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市) up to rev 90999208
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 鹿児島神社 |画像 = [[File:Kagosima jinja haiden.JPG|300px|拝殿]] |所在地 = 鹿児島県鹿児島市草牟田二丁目58番3号 |緯度度 = 31|緯度分 = 36|緯度秒 = 35.1 |経度度 =130|経度分 = 32|経度秒 = 22.6 |祭神 = 豊玉彦命
天津日高彦火火出見尊
豊玉姫命
豊受大命 |社格 = 国史見在社
旧県社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[切妻造]]平入 |別名 = 宇治（氏）瀬神社 |札所等 = 鹿児島三社 |例祭 = 10月18日・2月18日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Kagoshima city#Japan Kagoshima }} [[File:Sangoku meisyo zue Ujise shirine.jpg|thumb|江戸期の宇治瀬神社の絵図（[[三国名勝図会]]第三巻より）]] '''鹿児島神社'''（かごしまじんじゃ）は[[鹿児島県]][[鹿児島市]][[草牟田|草牟田二丁目]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。旧くは「'''宇治'''（'''氏'''）'''瀬神社'''」と称された[[国史見在社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]。鹿児島一円の[[地主神]]と伝えられ、藩政時代には[[鹿児島三社]]（[[一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|一之宮神社]]、鹿児島神社、[[川上天満宮]]）のうちの一社とされ、二之宮とも呼ばれていた。[[鹿児島県護国神社]]に隣接する。 == 祭神 == [[豊玉彦命]]、[[天津日高彦火火出見尊]]、[[豊玉姫命]]、[[豊受大神]]の4柱を祭神とする[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html 鹿児島神社] - 鹿児島県神社庁 2012年1月2日閲覧。。彦火火出見尊（[[山幸彦]]ともいう）と豊玉姫命は夫婦神、豊玉彦命は豊玉姫命の父神である海神の[[大綿津見神]]の別称とされる。 また、「開聞十八社の其一所」で、「祭る所は[[龍神]]たるべく、二月の祭より宇都瀬といい、十月より宇津佐とい」ったという『神道大辞典』所引『三国神社伝記』。。 == 由緒 == 社伝によると、[[彦火火出見尊]]（山幸彦）が綿津見宮に行かれた折、[[豊玉彦命]]は心を尽くして彦火火出見尊に仕えられたが、其の誠忠偉勲の功を称える為、彦火火出見尊の御子の[[鵜葺草葺不合命]]が創祀されたと伝えられる。 創建年代は不詳であるが、『[[日本三代実録]]』[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）3月20日条に「薩摩國従五位下鹿児島神に従五位上を授く。氏神は鹿児島の地主神なり」と記された[[薩摩国]]「'''鹿児島神'''」に充てられるので、平安前期以前に創建されていたのは間違いない（尚、関連が深い[[垂水市]]の[[鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|鹿児島神社]]は奈良時代末期の創建と伝わる）。 当神社は、往古は[[錦江湾]]に浮かぶ「神瀬の小島」なる島に鎮座していたといわれており、山手にありながら「海の神」として崇められてきたのもこの伝承を裏付けるものと考えられる。 当神社の石鳥居は、かつて[[桜島]]の袴腰地区にあった「神瀬の小島」の[[拝所|遥拝所]]の鳥居で、その当時のものと伝えられている。（現在は新しい鳥居に取り替えられており、この石鳥居は社殿の横の奥に置かれている） 旧名の「宇治（氏）瀬」とは、当神社のかつての鎮座地、[[錦江湾]]の「神瀬の小島」にかかる瀬のいと早く渦巻く様とも、また昔当神社前を流れていた[[甲突川]]のその早瀬の逆巻く様からきているともいわれている。 また、『[[三国名勝図会]]』によると[[甲突川]]という名前自体が、当神社の例祭の月（2月、10月）を「神月（こうつき）」と称したことに由来し、「神月川」から現在の名称に変わったという説もある。 古来上下から尊崇されたが、[[応永|応永年中]]（14世紀末から15世紀中頃）に[[島津元久]]によって社殿が建立されるなど[[島津氏]]による崇敬も篤く、藩政時代には[[島津忠恒|家久]]以下代々の[[薩摩藩|薩摩藩主]]から[[鹿児島三社]]（[[一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|一之宮神社]]、鹿児島神社、[[川上天満宮]]）のうちの一社（二之宮）とされ、[[家督]]を相続するとまず初めに当神社に参詣する例とされた。 [[安政]]6年（[[1859年]]）には正一位の位記が授けられた。[[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）に[[県社]]に列した。 「鹿児島神社」は[[垂水市]]にもあり（[[鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|鹿児島神社]]、別名「下宮神社」）、[[霧島市]]の[[鹿児島神宮]]も古名を「鹿児島神社」という。3つの鹿児島神社を直線で結ぶと[[桜島]]を囲む二等辺三角形になり、これらの神社は元々は[[桜島]]（古くは鹿児島と呼ばれたとする説がある）を[[神体]]として祀る神社だったとする説もある。 == 祭祀 == かつては2月朔から18日まで祭りが行われ、その間は領主、藩主といえども他国は勿論、他所へ赴くことも禁じられていたという。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 参考文献 == *『神社名鑑』、[[神社本庁]]、昭和38年 *『神道大辞典』（縮刷復刻版）、[[臨川書店]]、昭和44年 ISBN 4-653-01347-0（初版は[[平凡社]]刊、昭和12年） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かこしましんしや}} [[Category:鹿児島市の神社]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Kagoshima Shrine |image = [[File:Kagosima jinja haiden.JPG|300px|Haiden]] |Location = 2-chome-58-3 Sōmuta, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture |LatitudeDegrees = 31|LatitudeMinutes = 36|LatitudeSeconds = 35.1 |LongitudeDegrees = 130|LongitudeMinutes = 32|LongitudeSeconds = 22.6 |deity = Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto
Amatsuhidaka-Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto
Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto
Toyouke-no-Ōmikami |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造}}, Hirairi |Alias = Ujise Shrine |Fudasho = Kagoshima Sansha |Annual Festival = October 18 and February 18 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima city#Japan Kagoshima }} [[File:Sangoku meisyo zue Ujise shirine.jpg|thumb|Edo-period illustration of Ujise Shrine (from volume 3 of {{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会}})]] '''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sōmuta|ja|草牟田}}, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture. Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and was formerly Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ({{ill|Jinushigami|ja|地主神}}) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)}} |- |{{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=彦火火出見尊}} |yes |yes |yes |- |{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=豊玉姫尊}} |yes |yes |yes |- |[[Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}} |yes |no |no |- |[[Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=玉依姫尊}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=塩司翁・猿田彦命}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}} |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}" entry for March 20, 860 ({{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near {{ill|Sakurajima|ja|桜島}}'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}}, notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City}}, and {{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮}} in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzaisha]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto
Toyouke-no-Ōmikami |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft|12=draft}}
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|12=draft|12=draft}}, Hirairi |Alias = Ujise Shrine |Fudasho = Kagoshima Sansha |Annual Festival = October 18 and February 18 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima city#Japan Kagoshima }} [[File:Sangoku meisyo zue Ujise shirine.jpg|thumb|Edo-period illustration of Ujise Shrine (from volume 3 of {{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|12=draft|12=draft}})]] '''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sōmuta|ja|草牟田|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture. Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft|12=draft}} and was formerly Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ({{ill|Jinushigami|ja|地主神|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|12=draft|12=draft}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|12=draft|12=draft}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|12=draft|12=draft}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|12=draft|12=draft}} |- |{{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=彦火火出見尊|12=draft|12=draft}} |yes |yes |yes |- |{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=豊玉姫尊|12=draft|12=draft}} |yes |yes |yes |- |[[Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} |yes |no |no |- |[[Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=玉依姫尊|12=draft|12=draft}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=塩司翁・猿田彦命|12=draft|12=draft}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|12=draft|12=draft}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}" entry for March 20, 860 ({{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft}} era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|12=draft|12=draft}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near {{ill|Sakurajima|ja|桜島|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|12=draft|12=draft}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|12=draft|12=draft}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|12=draft|12=draft}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzaisha]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]]

visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}" entry for March 20, 860 ({{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkō Bay|qq=}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near {{ill|Sakurajima|ja|桜島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurajima|qq=}}'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtsuki River|qq=}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu clan|qq=}}, notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Jingū|qq=}} in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Kokushi-genzaisha]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]]

Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=}} and was formerly Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ({{ill|Jinushigami|ja|地主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinushigami|qq=}}) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami Tenmangū|qq=}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. The shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu clan|qq=}}, notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Jingū|qq=}} in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Kagoshima Shrine |image = [[File:Kagosima jinja haiden.JPG|300px|Haiden]] |Location = 2-chome-58-3 Sōmuta, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture |LatitudeDegrees = 31|LatitudeMinutes = 36|LatitudeSeconds = 35.1 |LongitudeDegrees = 130|LongitudeMinutes = 32|LongitudeSeconds = 22.6 |deity = Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto
Amatsuhidaka-Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto
Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto
Toyouke-no-Ōmikami |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=}}
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}}, Hirairi |Alias = Ujise Shrine |Fudasho = Kagoshima Sansha |Annual Festival = October 18 and February 18 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima city#Japan Kagoshima }} [[File:Sangoku meisyo zue Ujise shirine.jpg|thumb|Edo-period illustration of Ujise Shrine (from volume 3 of {{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}})]] '''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sōmuta|ja|草牟田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmuta|qq=}}, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture. Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=}} and was formerly Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ({{ill|Landlord deity|lt=Landlord deity|lt=Jinushigami|ja|地主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinushigami|qq=}}) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami Tenmangū|qq=}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamasachihiko|qq=}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Jingū|qq=}} !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |{{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|lt=Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}} |yes |yes |yes |- |{{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|トヨタマヒメ|lt=Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=豊玉姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=}} |yes |yes |yes |- |[[Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}} |yes |no |no |- |[[Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=玉依姫尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayoribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|qq=}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|lt=Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=塩司翁・猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}} |no |no |yes |- |{{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenji|qq=}} |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}" entry for March 20, 860 ({{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkō Bay|qq=}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near {{ill|Sakurajima|ja|桜島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurajima|qq=}}'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtsuki River|qq=}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu clan|qq=}}, notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kagoshima Jingū|ja|鹿児島神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Jingū|qq=}} in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]]

To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, established the shrine. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]]

Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto
Toyouke-no-Ōmikami |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=}}
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}}, Hirairi |Alias = Ujise Shrine |Fudasho = Kagoshima Sansha |Annual Festival = October 18 and February 18 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima city#Japan Kagoshima }} [[File:Sangoku meisyo zue Ujise shirine.jpg|thumb|Edo-period illustration of Ujise Shrine (from volume 3 of {{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}})]] '''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sōmuta|ja|草牟田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmuta|qq=}}, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture. Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=}} and was formerly Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ([[:en:Landlord deity|Jinushigami]]) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami Tenmangū|qq=}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamasachihiko|qq=}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}} |yes |no |no |- |[[Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" entry for March 20, 860 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkō Bay|qq=}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]]'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtsuki River|qq=}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]]

It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}} |yes |no |no |- |[[Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" entry for March 20, 860 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkō Bay|qq=}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]]'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtsuki River|qq=}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Kagoshima Shrine |image = [[File:Kagosima jinja haiden.JPG|300px|Haiden]] |Location = 2-chome-58-3 Sōmuta, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture |LatitudeDegrees = 31|LatitudeMinutes = 36|LatitudeSeconds = 35.1 |LongitudeDegrees = 130|LongitudeMinutes = 32|LongitudeSeconds = 22.6 |deity = Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto

Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=}} and was formerly Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ([[:en:Landlord deity|Jinushigami]]) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami Tenmangū|qq=}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. The shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima]] [[ja:鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Kagoshima Shrine |image = [[File:Kagosima jinja haiden.JPG|300px|Haiden]] |Location = 2-chome-58-3 Sōmuta, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture |LatitudeDegrees = 31|LatitudeMinutes = 36|LatitudeSeconds = 35.1 |LongitudeDegrees = 130|LongitudeMinutes = 32|LongitudeSeconds = 22.6 |deity = Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto
Amatsuhidaka-Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto
Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto
Toyouke-no-Ōmikami |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}}, Hirairi |Alias = Ujise Shrine |Fudasho = Kagoshima Sansha |Annual Festival = October 18 and February 18 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima city#Japan Kagoshima }} [[File:Sangoku meisyo zue Ujise shirine.jpg|thumb|Edo-period illustration of Ujise Shrine (from volume 3 of {{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}})]] '''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sōmuta|ja|草牟田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmuta|qq=}}, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture. Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and was formerly Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ([[:en:Landlord deity|Jinushigami]]) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami Tenmangū|qq=}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamasachihiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}} |yes |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" entry for March 20, 860 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkō Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿児島湾}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]]'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtsuki River|qq=}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima]] [[ja:鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)]]

Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamasachihiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife.
