Toyouke-no-Ōmikami |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}}, Hirairi |Alias = Ujise Shrine |Fudasho = Kagoshima Sansha |Annual Festival = October 18 and February 18 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima city#Japan Kagoshima }} [[File:Sangoku meisyo zue Ujise shirine.jpg|thumb|Edo-period illustration of Ujise Shrine (from volume 3 of {{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}})]] '''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sōmuta|ja|草牟田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmuta|qq=}}, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture. Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and was formerly Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ([[:en:Landlord deity|Jinushigami]]) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami Tenmangū|qq=}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamasachihiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}} |yes |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" entry for March 20, 860 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkō Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿児島湾}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]]'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtsuki River|qq=}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima]] [[ja:鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)]]

It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamasachihiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}} |yes |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. Considered the local guardian deity ([[:en:Landlord deity|Jinushigami]]) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami Tenmangū|qq=}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamasachihiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}} |yes |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" entry for March 20, 860 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkō Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿児島湾}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]]'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtsuki River|qq=}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shrines in Kagoshima]] [[ja:鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)]]

It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamasachihiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}} |yes |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamasachihiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}} |yes |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" entry for March 20, 860 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkō Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿児島湾}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]]'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtsuki River|qq=}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[ja:鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)]]

Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto
Toyouke-no-Ōmikami |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=}}, Hirairi |Alias = Ujise Shrine |Fudasho = Kagoshima Sansha |Annual Festival = October 18 and February 18 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima city#Japan Kagoshima }} [[File:Sangoku meisyo zue Ujise shirine.jpg|thumb|Edo-period illustration of Ujise Shrine (from volume 3 of {{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}})]] '''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sōmuta|ja|草牟田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmuta|qq=}}, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture. Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and was formerly Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ([[:en:Landlord deity|Jinushigami]]) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|qq=}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami Tenmangū|qq=}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamasachihiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}} |yes |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkō Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿児島湾}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]]'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtsuki River|qq=}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[ja:鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)]]

Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamasachihiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}} |yes |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" entry for March 20, 860 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkō Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿児島湾}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]]'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtsuki River|qq=}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[ja:鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)]]

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kagosima jinja haiden.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Sōmura|qid=Q11618203}}|coordinates={{Coord|31.60975|N|130.5396111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Hoori|qid=Q1051960}}; {{ill|Toyotama-hime|qid=Q2329261}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|qid=Q2983724}}; {{ill|Watatsumi|qid=Q1474187}}|architecture_style={{ill|rake-overhanging gabled roof|qid=Q13308575}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11676983]] Wikidata name: Kagoshima Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11676983]] Wikidata name: Kagoshima Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Kagoshima Shrine |image = [[File:Kagosima jinja haiden.JPG|300px|Haiden]] |Location = 2-chome-58-3 Sōmuta, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture |LatitudeDegrees = 31|LatitudeMinutes = 36|LatitudeSeconds = 35.1 |LongitudeDegrees = 130|LongitudeMinutes = 32|LongitudeSeconds = 22.6 |deity = Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto

Toyouke-no-Ōmikami |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}}, Hirairi |Alias = Ujise Shrine |Fudasho = Kagoshima Sansha |Annual Festival = October 18 and February 18 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima city#Japan Kagoshima }} [[File:Sangoku meisyo zue Ujise shirine.jpg|thumb|Edo-period illustration of Ujise Shrine (from volume 3 of {{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=|1=Sangoku meisho zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354726}})]] '''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sōmuta|ja|草牟田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmuta|qq=|1=Sōmura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618203}}, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture. Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and was formerly Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ([[:en:Landlord deity|Jinushigami]]) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|qq=|1=Ichinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352356}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami Tenmangū|qq=|1=Kawakami Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56347548}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=Kagoshima Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676849}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ|1=Toyotama-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2329261}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and was formerly Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ([[:en:Landlord deity|Jinushigami]]) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|qq=|1=Ichinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352356}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami Tenmangū|qq=|1=Kawakami Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56347548}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676849}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ|1=Toyotama-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2329261}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamasachihiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|1=Hoori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1051960}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} |yes |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373935}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" entry for March 20, 860 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkō Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿児島湾|1=Kagoshima Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1141442}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]]'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtsuki River|qq=|1=Kōtsuki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577963}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=|1=Sangoku meisho zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354726}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=|1=Shimazu Motohisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476798}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[ja:鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)]]

Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}}, Hirairi |Alias = Ujise Shrine |Fudasho = Kagoshima Sansha |Annual Festival = October 18 and February 18 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima city#Japan Kagoshima }} [[File:Sangoku meisyo zue Ujise shirine.jpg|thumb|Edo-period illustration of Ujise Shrine (from volume 3 of {{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=|1=Sangoku meisho zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354726}})]] '''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sōmuta|ja|草牟田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmuta|qq=|1=Sōmura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618203}}, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture. Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and was formerly Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ([[:en:Landlord deity|Jinushigami]]) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|qq=|1=Ichinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352356}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami Tenmangū|qq=|1=Kawakami Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56347548}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676849}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ|1=Toyotama-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2329261}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamasachihiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|1=Hoori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1051960}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} |yes |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373935}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" entry for March 20, 860 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkō Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿児島湾|1=Kagoshima Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1141442}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]]'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtsuki River|qq=|1=Kōtsuki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577963}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=|1=Sangoku meisho zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354726}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=|1=Shimazu Motohisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476798}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[ja:鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11676983}}

In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[ja:鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11676983}}

Amatsuhidaka-Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto
Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto
Toyouke-no-Ōmikami |Rank = {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}
Former Prefectural Shrine |Founded = Unknown |Honden = {{ill|Kirizuma-zukuri|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-zukuri|qq=|1=rake-overhanging gabled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13308575}}, Hirairi |Alias = Ujise Shrine |Fudasho = Kagoshima Sansha |Annual Festival = October 18 and February 18 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Kagoshima city#Japan Kagoshima }} [[File:Sangoku meisyo zue Ujise shirine.jpg|thumb|Edo-period illustration of Ujise Shrine (from volume 3 of {{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=|1=Sangoku meisho zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354726}})]] '''Kagoshima Shrine''' (鹿児島神社, ''Kagoshima Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Sōmuta|ja|草牟田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōmuta|qq=|1=Sōmura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618203}}, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture. Historically known as '''Ujise Shrine''', it is listed as a {{ill|Kokushi-genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi-genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and was formerly Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine. Considered the local guardian deity ([[:en:Landlord deity|Jinushigami]]) of the Kagoshima area, it was one of the Kagoshima Sansha shrines (alongside {{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|ja|一之宮神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya Shrine (Kagoshima)|qq=|1=Ichinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352356}} and {{ill|Kawakami Tenmangū|ja|川上天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakami Tenmangū|qq=|1=Kawakami Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56347548}}), also referred to as Ninomiya. It is located next to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|ja|鹿児島県護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefectural Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=Kagoshima Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676849}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines four kami: {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}}, {{ill|Hoori|ja|天津日高彦火火出見尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ|1=Toyotama-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2329261}}, and {{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}[http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/kagoshima/kagoshima/post-246.html Kagoshima Shrine] - Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Association, accessed January 2, 2012.. Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto (also known as {{ill|Yamasachihiko|ja|山幸彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamasachihiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ|1=Hoori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1051960}}) and Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto are husband and wife. Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto is another name for {{ill|Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|ja|大綿津見神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwatatsumi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワタツミ|1=Watatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1474187}}, the sea god and father of Toyotamahime. It is also noted as one of the eighteen shrines of Kaimon, and "the deity worshipped is likely a {{ill|Ryūjin|ja|龍神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūjin|qq=}}; the February festival is called 'Utsuse' and the October festival 'Utsusa'."Cited in "Shinto Daijiten" from "Sangoku Jinja Denki". {| class="wikitable" !Deity !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Kagoshima City)|qq=}} ![[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] !{{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}} |- |[[:en:Hoori|Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotamahime-no-Mikoto]] |yes |yes |yes |- |[[:en:Watatsumi|Watatsumi]] |yes |no |no |- |{{ill|Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-no-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} |yes |no |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] |no |yes |no |- |[[Nakatsuhime]] |no |yes |no |- |[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayoribime-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Shioshiji-no-Okina, Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] |no |no |yes |- |[[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]] |no |no |yes |- |Ōmiya-hime |no |no |yes |} == History == According to shrine tradition, when Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto visited Watatsumi's palace, Toyotamahiko-no-Mikoto served him with great devotion. To honor this sincere devotion, Hikohodemi-no-Mikoto's child, {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|ja|鵜葺草葺不合命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウガヤフキアエズ|1=Ugayafukiaezu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373935}}, established the shrine. The exact founding date is unknown, but the shrine appears in the "[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" entry for March 20, 860 ([[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] era), as "Kagoshima-no-Kami," indicating its existence since at least the early Heian period (another closely related Kagoshima Shrine in Tarumizu City dates from the late Nara period). The shrine is believed to have originally been located on "Kamise no Kojima," a small island in {{ill|Kinkō Bay|ja|錦江湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkō Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿児島湾|1=Kagoshima Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1141442}}, reinforcing its connection to sea worship despite its current inland location. The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]]'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtsuki River|qq=|1=Kōtsuki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577963}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|qq=|1=Sangoku meisho zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354726}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|qq=|1=Shimazu Motohisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476798}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[ja:鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11676983}}

The shrine's stone torii was originally from the remote worship site for "Kamise no Kojima" near [[:en:Sakurajima|Sakurajima]]'s Hakamagoshi district. This historic torii now resides beside the main shrine building. The original name "Ujise" references either the rapid whirlpools of Kinkō Bay or the swift currents of the {{ill|Kōtsuki River|ja|甲突川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtsuki River|1=Kōtsuki River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577963}}, which once flowed in front of the shrine. According to "{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|1=Sangoku meisho zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354726}}," the name "Kōtsuki River" itself may derive from "Kōtsuki" (神月, "God's Month"), referencing the shrine's festival months (February and October). The shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]], notably rebuilt by {{ill|Shimazu Motohisa|ja|島津元久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu Motohisa|1=Shimazu Motohisa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11476798}} in the early 15th century, and revered by generations of Satsuma domain lords, who traditionally visited the shrine upon succession. In 1859 (Ansei 6), the shrine received the Rank of Shoichi-i. In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a Prefectural Shrine. Notably, three shrines named Kagoshima Shrine—this shrine, {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|ja|鹿児島神社 (垂水市)|lt=another in Tarumizu City|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Shrine (Tarumizu City)|qq=}}, and [[:en:Kagoshima Shrine|Kagoshima Jingū]] in Kirishima City—form an isosceles triangle around Sakurajima, suggesting an ancient tradition of worshipping Sakurajima itself as a deity. == Rituals == Historically, festivities from February 1st to 18th strictly prohibited lords from traveling elsewhere during that period. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Jinja Meikan," Jinja Honchō, 1963 * "Shinto Daijiten" (reduced reprint), Rinsen Shoten, 1969 ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (originally published by Heibonsha, 1937) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)|version=90999208|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] }} [[ja:鹿児島神社_(鹿児島市)]] [[ja:鹿児島神社]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima]] [[ja:鹿児島神社 (鹿児島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11676983}}
